depression.description: Every time I make a mistake, it feels like I'm under a microscope. The constant criticism makes me question my abilities and leaves me feeling helpless.
I often feel like I'm not good enough at my job. No matter how hard I try, I can't shake the belief that I'm incompetent.

depression.topic: Fear of micromanagement and its impact
depression.description: The micromanagement at work makes me feel trapped. I worry that my efforts are never enough, and it just adds to my sadness and loneliness.
I find myself constantly thinking about past mistakes at work. It's like a loop in my mind that reinforces my feelings of being inadequate.
When I'm blamed for something I didn't do, I feel anxious and scared. It's hard to explain my side without feeling frustrated and discouraged.

Delusion.topic: Belief in needing approval from colleagues
Delusion.description: I feel that I must be acknowledged and treated fairly by my colleagues to prove my competence. If they don't recognize my efforts, I start to believe I'm failing.
I feel like I have to take responsibility for everything at work, even things that aren't my fault. It's exhausting and makes me feel more trapped.
